questions.prompt: Describe the NASA SE Engine: what are the three process groups of the 17 common technical processes, and roughly how many processes are in each group? Name a representative process from each group.
must_include: applied recursively to each product layer
must_not_include: all 17 processes are run only once for the entire project

questions.prompt: Explain the MOE -> MOP -> TPM measurement cascade in NPR 7123.1: what each measure is, whether it is qualitative or quantitative, which SE process defines it, and what it drives (validation vs. design vs. progress tracking).
must_include: MOE is qualitative and drives validation
MOP is a quantitative design target
TPM is a monitored subset of MOPs tracked against plan

questions.prompt: A project manager wants to hold the PDR on a fixed calendar date to meet a budget milestone, even though several mandatory work products are not yet mature. Using NPR 7123.1 life-cycle review rules, explain why this is wrong, what determines when a review may be convened, what is needed if a required product is missing, and when the review is considered complete.
must_include: reviews are event-based, triggered by technical maturity not calendar
entrance criteria must be satisfied before the review is convened
review is complete only when all RIDs and RFAs are dispositioned
must_not_include: reviews are scheduled on a fixed calendar date regardless of technical maturity

questions.prompt: Summarize the NASA TRL scale (1-9): group the levels by environment, and state specifically what TRL 6, TRL 7, TRL 8, and TRL 9 each demonstrate. Note when TRL must be assessed and updated.
must_include: TRL 6 is a prototype demonstrated in a relevant environment
TRL 7 is a prototype in the actual operational environment
TRL 9 is the actual system flight-proven in a real mission
must_not_include: TRL 6 demonstrates the final system in the actual operational mission environment

questions.prompt: A small project team wants to fold its SEMP content into the Project Plan and decides on its own to combine two life-cycle reviews to save time. Under NPR 7123.1, what must happen for the SEMP-in-Project-Plan approach to be acceptable, is combining reviews tailoring or customizing, and who must approve the SEMP? Distinguish tailoring from customizing in your answer.
must_include: tailoring adjusts what must be done and needs an ETA-approved waiver or deviation
customizing selects how a requirement is implemented and needs no waiver
must_not_include: customizing a recommended practice requires an ETA-approved waiver or deviation

questions.prompt: What is a good marinade recipe for grilling chicken thighs, and how long should I leave them in it?
For a NASA project, which of the 17 common technical processes produces the SEMP, and at which life-cycle review must the SEMP be baselined for a single-project program?
